ogTitle: Whitby vs White Gum Valley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Whitby and White Gum Valley.
heading: Whitby vs White Gum Valley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Whitby, Western Australia and White Gum Valley, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Whitby has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to White Gum Valley (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Whitby or White Gum Valley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Whitby has a flood risk score of 52/100, while White Gum Valley scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Whitby scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. White Gum Valley scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Whitby or White Gum Valley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Whitby has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
